transcript.text: The bills keep piling up and I keep checking the bank account, but my partner says I’m always at the office and doesn’t notice. I feel anxious all the time, even when I try to focus on the numbers. I know I need to make a budget, but the pressure is overwhelming.

You're dealing with a lot of financial stress, and it's taking a toll on your daily life. Your partner's comment about you being at the office a lot is probably not helping, making you feel like you're shouldering this burden alone. The anxiety you're feeling is understandable, given the pressure to make a budget and get your finances in order. It sounds like you're feeling really stuck right now, and that's a tough spot to be in. Yeah, it can be overwhelming to try to tackle all of this by yourself.
Yeah, I hear you. The endless work hours make me feel like I'm drowning, and my partner's frustration just adds to the anxiety. I keep thinking I need to fix everything, but I don't know how.

The pressure to fix everything and the endless work hours are really taking a toll on you, making it hard to catch your breath. It's like the weight of responsibility is crushing you, and your partner's frustration is just adding to the anxiety. Yeah, it's overwhelming to feel like you're shouldering this burden alone. I can see why you'd feel like you're drowning in all of this.

I keep calculating the monthly debt payment and the repair costs, and it just feels like the numbers keep growing. My partner keeps telling me I’m ignoring her, but I’m already buried in invoices. I’m scared my guilt is turning into a full-blown anxiety spiral, and my parents are still waiting for the medical bills to get sorted. I don’t know how to balance all of this without losing my head.

The weight of these responsibilities is crushing you, and it's no wonder you're scared of losing control. Your partner's frustration and your own guilt are adding to the anxiety, making it hard to see a way out. It's like the numbers and the bills are just piling up, and you're shouldering this burden alone. Yeah, it's overwhelming to feel like you're drowning in all of this.

I know I should talk to my parents about the bills, but I’m scared that the conversation will just add to their stress, and my partner keeps saying I’m ignoring her. Every time I try to focus on the numbers, I end up feeling more anxious and like I’m drowning at work. I feel like I’m failing on all fronts.

The pressure to manage your finances, deal with your partner's frustration, and worry about your parents' stress is taking a huge toll on you. It's like you're being pulled in multiple directions, and it's hard to know where to start. Your fear of talking to your parents about the bills is understandable, given how overwhelming everything feels right now. Yeah, it's a lot to handle, and it's no wonder you're feeling anxious and like you're failing on all fronts.

I pulled all the bills into one spreadsheet, but my partner still says I’m ignoring her, and I keep feeling like I’m drowning in numbers. I know I should bring this up with my parents, but I’m scared the conversation will just add to their stress. I keep thinking I’m failing at everything, which makes the anxiety feel even worse.

You've taken a big step by pulling all the bills into one spreadsheet, but it's clear that the stress and anxiety are still really getting to you. Your partner's comment about feeling ignored is probably not helping, and the fear of talking to your parents about the bills is understandable. It's like you're being pulled in multiple directions, and it's hard to know where to start. Yeah, it's a lot to handle, and it's no wonder you're feeling anxious and like you're failing on all fronts. I can see why you'd feel overwhelmed by all of this.

I pulled the spreadsheet, but I still feel like I’m drowning in numbers. My partner’s frustration feels like another item on the list, and I’m terrified that talking to my parents will just add to their stress. I’m scared I’m failing on every front, and that just makes the anxiety worse.

You've been working hard to get a handle on the bills, but it's clear that the stress and anxiety are still really getting to you. It's like every new task or conversation feels like another weight on your shoulders. What's been the hardest part of dealing with your partner's frustration and the pressure to talk to your parents about the bills?

The hardest part is that when I try to explain the bills, she thinks I'm ignoring her, and when I talk to my parents it just feels like adding more weight. I feel like I'm failing at everything, and the anxiety is just spiraling.

The bills and your partner's frustration are piling up, and it's like every conversation feels like a potential landmine. When you try to explain the bills, you feel like you're being pulled in two directions - trying to get your partner to understand, but also feeling like you're ignoring her. And talking to your parents about the bills just feels like adding more weight to an already overwhelming situation. Yeah, it's no wonder the anxiety is spiraling. What do you think would happen if you could find a way to communicate with your partner about the bills that didn't feel like you were ignoring her?

I guess if I could talk to her about the bills without feeling like I'm ignoring her, maybe she'd understand I'm juggling a lot. But every time I bring it up, she thinks I'm avoiding her. I’m not sure I can do that.

You're trying to find a way to talk to your partner about the bills without feeling like you're ignoring her, but it's hard when she thinks you're avoiding her. It sounds like you're feeling really overwhelmed by all the different things you're trying to juggle. What if you could find a way to explain the bills that felt more like a team effort, rather than something you're handling alone? Maybe that would help her understand that you're not ignoring her, but rather trying to manage a lot of different responsibilities.

I keep trying to explain the bills, but she just tells me I'm ignoring her. I feel like I'm failing and that makes my anxiety jump up. Maybe if I frame it as a team effort, but I don't even know how to say that without sounding like I'm avoiding her.

It's really tough to have this conversation with your partner when you feel like you're being misunderstood. You've been trying to explain the bills, but it's like you're speaking different languages. Maybe if you could find a way to frame it as a team effort, like you mentioned, that could help her understand that you're not ignoring her. What do you think is the hardest part of having this conversation, and how can you start to feel more like you're on the same team?

Every time I try to bring up the bills she says I'm ignoring her, so I feel like I'm failing and I just want to avoid the whole thing. That makes me anxious all the time, and I keep wondering if I'm just blowing this up. I wish there was a way to explain it that doesn't sound like I'm avoiding her.

It's really tough to have this conversation with your partner when you feel like you're being misunderstood, and it's even harder when you feel like you're failing. You've been trying to explain the bills, but it's like you're not being heard, and that's probably making you feel even more anxious and overwhelmed. It sounds like you're feeling really stuck and unsure of how to move forward. Yeah, it's a lot to handle, and it's no wonder you're feeling anxious all the time.
